message:
fix(walker,voi,pid): improve signal detection for symlinks, incremental forecast errors, safe duration cast

Three targeted fixes across the scanner and monitoring subsystems:

Walker:
- Move exclusion check before .sbh-protect marker check for early bail-out
- Move symlink skip AFTER signal collection so symlinked .git/Cargo.toml
  still counts as a structural signal for the parent directory
- Replace raw byte-suffix extension matching with Path::extension() +
  eq_ignore_ascii_case() for robustness
- Add signals_detects_symlinked_markers test (unix-only)

VoI Scheduler:
- Accumulate forecast errors incrementally during record_scan_result()
  via new pending_errors field instead of snapshot-recomputing in end_window()
- Remove premature end_window() call from daemon loop scan handler
- Use sort_by with path tie-breaker for deterministic plan ordering

PID Monitor:
- Replace clippy lint suppression with explicit .min(u64::MAX) clamp
  to prevent truncation on very large durations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
